title: Vulpia linneana
intro: Vulpia linneana, also known as hairy cat's ear, is a perennial grass native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia, characterized by its hairy leaves and purplish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Vulpia linneana include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vulpia linneana include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, often used to alleviate respiratory symptoms and support immune function.
taxonomy: Vulpia linneana is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Vulpia, which consists of annual grasses native to Europe and Asia. The genus Vulpia is further divided into several species, and Vulpia linneana is one of them. Vulpia linneana is also known as sheep's fescue or sheep's furze. It is typically found in dry, rocky areas and sandy soils, particularly in the Mediterranean region.
